command.text: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -name '*.txt' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -w | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print sum/(NR-1)}'

raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to compute mean average of word count of *.txt files in /testbed. Use wc -w and average. Command: awk. For example: wc -w /testbed/*.txt | awk 'END{print $1/NR}'. But wc outputs each file and total. Actually wc -w gives per file and total. We can use find and xargs. Simpler: wc -w /testbed/*.txt | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print sum/NR-1}'. But better: use find and wc -w | awk. Let's produce: find /testbed -maxdepth 1 -name '*.txt' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -w | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print sum/(NR-1)}'. That gives mean. Provide command.
